title: 88 million dollars with open source: Ollama enters 85% of the Fortune 500, calling for everyone to get on board
excerpt: A single command can bring up an open-source large model locally. This tool, which has freed countless developers from API keys and exorbitant bills, has just s
content_html:
A single command can bring up an open-source large model locally. This tool, which has freed countless developers from API keys and exorbitant bills, has just secured the fuel for its next phase. On July 9, Ollama announced in its official blog that it has completed an $88 million funding round. The investor list spans top-tier venture capital and veterans of the open-source world: Benchmark's Peter Fenton, Theory Ventures' Tomasz Tunguz, and 8VC's Alex Kolicich led the round, with Solomon Hykes, founder of Docker, Aaron Katz, CEO of ClickHouse, Spencer Kimball, co-founder of GIMP and co-founder of Cockroach Labs, Quinn Slack, CEO of Amp, Marianna Tessel, a Cisco board member, Michael Montano, former engineering head at Twitter, as well as Y Combinator, Garage Capital, Pace Capital, 49 Palms, and GTMFund among others as angel investors.

The company's founders, Jeff and Michael, have been doing the same thing for ten years. The two met in college and founded Kitematic, which made running Docker extremely simple. In 2015, Docker acquired it, and their work later became Docker Desktop, now used by over ten million developers worldwide. Ten years later, they applied the same philosophy of making complex technology accessible to all to AI—Ollama's goal is to allow developers to start and run open-source models with the least effort. Beyond their wildest imagination, this platform has already served 8.9 million developers and is adopted by 85% of Fortune 500 companies.

In Ollama's narrative, open-source models are ushering in a PC-like moment for AI: just as PCs brought computing power from mainframe rooms to every desktop, allowing people to own, customize, and build upon them, open-source models are doing the same today. A few years ago, powerful and free open-source models already existed, but getting them to run smoothly was fraught with obstacles. The capability was there, yet it felt locked behind a door, making it difficult for developers to use them as easily as calling a proprietary model's API. Ollama's solution is an application you can download to your computer, starting the latest open-source model with a single command and then building on it through a simple API, making running open-source models as easy as running any ordinary software—without needing permissions, API keys, or expensive server hardware. Your model, your machine, your data.

Its product is built around three principles: ownership, affordability, and privacy. Ownership means open-source models belong to the user, who can keep, customize, and optimize them anytime without being locked into a specific model that their agent or application depends on. Affordability comes from running models on your own hardware, eliminating runaway billing based on tokens, so experiments, iterations, and releases no longer need to worry about each prompt adding to the cost. Privacy ensures data never leaves your local machine, and even if it does go to the cloud for expansion, the same level of trust can be carried along.

Open-source models are no longer just toys in laboratories. Ollama Cloud has become the most convenient one-stop access to the most powerful open-source models, including GLM, Nemotron, DeepSeek, Kimi, MiniMax, and more. Its token usage grows by more than 100% monthly on average. What began as the joy of running a model for the first time on a personal computer has now expanded to tackle the tough challenges once reserved for closed-source models.

This $88 million has been positioned by Ollama as fuel to push the ecosystem forward. It is currently at the core of the open-source model ecosystem, and the funds will be invested in three areas: seamless hybrid reasoning, support for newly released open-source models on the day they are launched, and a cloud service that allows any developer and their team to use the most powerful models without sacrificing ownership or privacy. Jeff and Michael wrote in the conclusion that they had stood at the beginning of similar changes before, betting that openness and ease of use would ultimately prevail. Now, they are putting everything on the line again. With 85% of Fortune 500 companies quietly moving open-source models into their internal systems, the next carriage on this open-source train clearly still has plenty of seats available.

sentences.zh: 可承受性来自于在自己的硬件上运行模型，消除了基于代币的失控计费，因此实验、迭代和发布不再需要担心每次提示都会增加成本。

sentences.zh: 隐私确保数据永远不会离开您的本地计算机，即使它确实转移到云端进行扩展，也可以保持相同级别的信任。

sentences.en: Open-source models are no longer just toys in laboratories.

sentences.en: Ollama Cloud has become the most convenient one-stop access to the most powerful open-source models, including GLM, Nemotron, DeepSeek, Kimi, MiniMax, and more.
sentences.zh: Ollama Cloud 已成为最便捷的一站式访问最强大的开源模型的方式，包括 GLM、Nemotron、DeepSeek、Kimi、MiniMax 等。

sentences.en: Its token usage grows by more than 100% monthly on average.
sentences.zh: 其代币使用量每月平均增长超过 100%。

sentences.en: What began as the joy of running a model for the first time on a personal computer has now expanded to tackle the tough challenges once reserved for closed-source models.
sentences.zh: 最初是第一次在个人计算机上运行模型的乐趣，现在已经扩展到解决曾经为闭源模型保留的严峻挑战。

blocks.html_en: This $88 million has been positioned by Ollama as fuel to push the ecosystem forward. It is currently at the core of the open-source model ecosystem, and the funds will be invested in three areas: seamless hybrid reasoning, support for newly released open-source models on the day they are launched, and a cloud service that allows any developer and their team to use the most powerful models without sacrificing ownership or privacy. Jeff and Michael wrote in the conclusion that they had stood at the beginning of similar changes before, betting that openness and ease of use would ultimately prevail. Now, they are putting everything on the line again. With 85% of Fortune 500 companies quietly moving open-source models into their internal systems, the next carriage on this open-source train clearly still has plenty of seats available.

sentences.en: This $88 million has been positioned by Ollama as fuel to push the ecosystem forward.
sentences.zh: Ollama 将这 8800 万美元定位为推动生态系统向前发展的燃料。

sentences.en: It is currently at the core of the open-source model ecosystem, and the funds will be invested in three areas: seamless hybrid reasoning, support for newly released open-source models on the day they are launched, and a cloud service that allows any developer and their team to use the most powerful models without sacrificing ownership or privacy.
sentences.zh: 它目前处于开源模型生态系统的核心，资金将投资于三个领域：无缝混合推理、对新发布的开源模型在发布当天的支持，以及允许任何开发者及其团队在不牺牲所有权或隐私的情况下使用最强大的模型的云服务。

sentences.en: Jeff and Michael wrote in the conclusion that they had stood at the beginning of similar changes before, betting that openness and ease of use would ultimately prevail.
sentences.zh: 杰夫和迈克尔在结论中写道，他们之前也曾经历过类似的变革，并坚信开放性和易用性最终会占上风。

sentences.en: Now, they are putting everything on the line again.
sentences.zh: 现在，他们再次将一切都置于危险之中。

sentences.en: With 85% of Fortune 500 companies quietly moving open-source models into their internal systems, the next carriage on this open-source train clearly still has plenty of seats available.
sentences.zh: 随着 85% 的财富 500 强公司悄悄地将开源模型转移到其内部系统中，这趟开源列车的下一节车厢显然仍然有足够的座位。

content_html_zh:
一条命令就可以在本地启动一个开源大型模型。 这个工具让无数开发者摆脱了 API 密钥和高昂的账单，刚刚为下一阶段提供了燃料。 7月9日，Ollama在官方博客宣布完成8800万美元融资。 投资者名单涵盖顶级风险投资和开源领域的资深人士：Benchmark 的 Peter Fenton、Theory Ventures 的 Tomasz Tunguz 和 8VC 的 Alex Kolicich 领投，Docker 创始人 Solomon Hykes、ClickHouse 首席执行官 Aaron Katz、GIMP 联合创始人兼 Cockroach Labs 联合创始人 Spencer Kimball、Amp 首席执行官 Quinn Slack、Marianna思科董事会成员 Tessel、Twitter 前工程主管 Michael Montano，以及 Y Combinator、Garage Capital、Pace Capital、49 Palms 和 GTMFund 等天使投资人。

该公司的创始人杰夫和迈克尔十年来一直在做同样的事情。 两人在大学相识并创立了 Kitematic，这使得运行 Docker 变得异常简单。 2015 年，Docker 收购了它，他们的作品后来成为 Docker Desktop，现在全球有超过一千万开发者在使用。 十年后，他们应用了同样的理念，即让所有人都能使用复杂的技术来人工智能——Ollama 的目标是让开发人员以最少的努力启动和运行开源模型。 超出他们最疯狂的想象，这个平台已经服务了 890 万开发者，并被 85% 的财富 500 强公司采用。

在 Ollama 的叙述中，开源模型正在为人工智能迎来一个类似 PC 的时刻：正如 PC 将计算能力从大型机房带到每个桌面，允许人们拥有、定制和构建它们一样，开源模型今天也在做同样的事情。 几年前，强大且免费的开源模型已经存在，但要让它们顺利运行却充满了障碍。 该功能已经存在，但感觉被锁在了门后，使得开发人员很难像调用专有模型的 API 那样轻松地使用它们。 Ollama 的解决方案是一个可以下载到计算机上的应用程序，使用单个命令启动最新的开源模型，然后通过简单的 API 进行构建，使运行开源模型像运行任何普通软件一样简单，无需权限、API 密钥或昂贵的服务器硬件。 您的模型、您的机器、您的数据。

其产品围绕三个原则构建：所有权、负担能力和隐私。 所有权意味着开源模型属于用户，用户可以随时保留、定制和优化它们，而不必局限于其代理或应用程序所依赖的特定模型。 可承受性来自于在自己的硬件上运行模型，消除了基于代币的失控计费，因此实验、迭代和发布不再需要担心每次提示都会增加成本。 隐私确保数据永远不会离开您的本地计算机，即使它确实转移到云端进行扩展，也可以保持相同级别的信任。

开源模型不再只是实验室里的玩具。 Ollama Cloud 已成为最便捷的一站式访问最强大的开源模型的方式，包括 GLM、Nemotron、DeepSeek、Kimi、MiniMax 等。 其代币使用量每月平均增长超过 100%。 最初是第一次在个人计算机上运行模型的乐趣，现在已经扩展到解决曾经为闭源模型保留的严峻挑战。

Ollama 将这 8800 万美元定位为推动生态系统向前发展的燃料。 它目前处于开源模型生态系统的核心，资金将投资于三个领域：无缝混合推理、对新发布的开源模型在发布当天的支持，以及允许任何开发者及其团队在不牺牲所有权或隐私的情况下使用最强大的模型的云服务。 杰夫和迈克尔在结论中写道，他们之前也曾经历过类似的变革，并坚信开放性和易用性最终会占上风。 现在，他们再次将一切都置于危险之中。 随着 85% 的财富 500 强公司悄悄地将开源模型转移到其内部系统中，这趟开源列车的下一节车厢显然仍然有足够的座位。
